title: Linting for Correctness, Safety, and Maintainability · Broken-Build Bay
summary: Configure a high-signal linter beyond default style warnings. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a risk-based lint policy and fixtures and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
Set severity from failure impact and confidence rather than preference strength.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Lint Upgrades evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Lint Upgrades assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Lint Upgrades, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a risk-based lint policy and fixtures.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Linter upgrades are behavior-free dependency bumps. This fails the Lint Upgrades contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: TypeScript TSConfig Reference (TypeScript 5.9) — Controls strict typing, unused code checks, indexed access, and compiler gate behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-lint-upgrades-o5kgca: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Lint Upgrades into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-lint-upgrades-o5kgca:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Lint Upgrades precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Lint Upgrades applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Recommended Baseline to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Recommended Baseline.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
